# <Initiative / Feature Name> — BRD Draft

> Clarify draft at **business altitude**. Items marked `ASSUMPTION` /
> `OPEN QUESTION` / `SUGGESTION` need human confirmation. Clarify does not invent
> business rules. Deep technical mechanics are deferred to §15 Downstream
> Technical Notes — they are not BRD scope.

## 5. Product Variant / Options Matrix (for confirmation)
Proactively proposed options for the user to choose — **not** an assumed choice.
Pick one (or more) by ID in the Answer Sheet (§16).
| ID | Variant | Key differentiators | … |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| V1 | <option A> | <…> | <…> |
| V2 | <option B> | <…> | <…> |

## 6. High-level Business Process
Textual flow(s) at business altitude (diagrams optional — defer detailed
PlantUML/Mermaid to the PRD).
```text
<Actor> <does step> → <system/business outcome> → <next step> → …
```

### 6.1 Customer Journey (end-to-end)
The ordered steps the user takes from entry to result — include the steps before
and after the core action, and the authentication/confirmation step.
1. <enters via menu / lands on …>
2. <chooses option / sees terms & key info>
3. <enters details / selects source & options>
4. <reviews preview: amount, dates, expected outcome>
5. <accepts terms> → <authenticates: OTP/PIN/biometric>
6. <system processes> → <result: success/failure>
7. <views detail / next action>

### 6.2 Screen Information / Display Matrix
What each key screen must show and let the user do (information level, not visual
design). Include loading, empty, validation, and error states where the user may
be blocked or confused.
| Screen name | Purpose | Display fields | User actions | Validation | Error / empty / loading states | UX / content notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <product list> | <help user choose> | <product, rate/key terms, eligibility> | <select / compare> | <eligible products only?> | <no products / loading / unavailable> | <plain-language labels> |
| <input> | <collect details> | <amount, source, term/options> | <enter / select / continue> | <min/max, source allowed, required fields> | <invalid input / insufficient permission> | <show helper copy> |
| <confirmation summary> | <confirm commitment> | <amount, source, terms, dates, expected result, fees/penalty, terms checkbox> | <accept terms / authenticate / cancel> | <terms accepted, auth required> | <rate changed / auth unavailable> | <no technical jargon> |
| <result> | <show outcome> | <success/failure/pending, reference, next step> | <view detail / retry / contact support> | <n/a> | <unknown / processing / failed> | <message matches error map> |
| <detail> | <review record after completion> | <principal, rate, status, dates, actions> | <download / manage / close> | <action eligibility> | <record not found / pending> | <show business status> |

## 13. Risks, Dependencies & Compliance
- Risk / dependency: <…>
- Compliance / regulatory: <…>

### 13.1 Error Handling & Customer Messages (summary)
Key failures with the **message the customer sees**, the state left behind, and
the next action (full code/status table in `error-handling.md`). User messages
avoid technical jargon.
| Error code | Flow / Step | Scenario | Entity state | Transaction / operation state | User-facing message | Retryable? | Required action | Needs Ops/CS? |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <CODE_001> | <F01 / step 3> | <insufficient balance> | <unchanged / none created> | <failed> | <plain message> | yes | <choose another source / amount> | no |
| <CODE_002> | <F01 / auth step> | <auth failed / expired / cancelled> | <unchanged / pending> | <failed / cancelled> | <plain message> | yes/no | <retry / resend / restart> | no |
| <CODE_003> | <F01 / processing> | <money moved but record not created> | <pending / unknown> | <processing / unknown / needs review> | <"being processed; if funds moved, status will be updated or corrected"> | no | <check status / contact support> | yes |

### 13.2 State Summary
For process / transaction features, distinguish the state of the business object
from the state of the action currently being processed. Mark unknowns as
`OPEN QUESTION`.
| Model type | State set | Key transitions / terminal states | Gaps |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Entity state | <e.g. pending, active, closed> | <allowed transitions> | <OPEN QUESTION / none> |
| Transaction / operation state | <initiated, pending-auth, processing, success, failed, timeout, reversed, unknown> | <auth → processing → final state> | <OPEN QUESTION / none> |

## 14. Success Metrics / KPIs
- <measurable target, e.g. activation rate, accounts opened, balance/AUM, flow completion rate, failed-transaction rate>

## 15. Downstream Technical Notes (for PRD / SRS — not BRD scope)
> Surfaced for traceability so nothing is lost; to be detailed in the PRD/SRS.
> Business reviewers may skip this section.
- Configuration mechanics: effective-dated/versioned config rows, validation, propagation.
- Scheduled/batch jobs: idempotency keys, resume-on-failure, outputs, downstream consumers.
- Data integrity: e.g. double-entry ledger, source of truth, reconciliation mechanics.
- API / event / integration impact.

## 16. Answer Sheet (copy, fill in, send back)
Copy this block, fill after each ID, and paste it back — `improve` applies it by ID.
```text
# Variant — pick one id (see §5):
Variant: V1
# Assumptions — keep | override: <value>
A1: keep
A2: keep
# Open questions — your answer (or "skip"):
Q1:
Q2:
# Suggestions — yes | no | later:
S1:
S2:
```
